title: SVG Text on a Path
summary: Text that flows along a curve, optionally scrolling around it — circular badges and arched headings.
description: Uses <textPath href='#path'> to lay text along an arbitrary path, with an animated startOffset to make it travel — perfect for circular seal/badge text and curved labels.
browser_support: All modern browsers (textPath). startOffset animation via SMIL works broadly.
performance_notes: Cheap. Define the path once and reference it; hide the path stroke if you only want the text.
accessibility_notes: SVG text is real, selectable, and readable by screen readers — better than image text. Add role=img + aria-label on the SVG if the curved text is part of a logo. SMIL motion ignores reduced-motion; the script pauses it.

customization.name: path d
customization.description: The curve the text follows (arc, circle, wave)

customization.name: startOffset
customization.description: Where text begins along the path (animate to scroll)

variations: Text along a wavy or spiral path
ai_usage: Use for circular badges/seals, arched titles, and curved labels. Define a <path id='p'> (a circle drawn as a path for a ring of text, or an arc for an arch) and put <text><textPath href='#p'>…</textPath></text>. Set the path's stroke to none if you only want text. To rotate the text around the ring, animate startOffset 0%→100% (SMIL) — pause under reduced motion. SVG text stays accessible/selectable.
source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>SVG Text on a Path</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "svg-text-on-path",
    "title": "SVG Text on a Path",
    "summary": "Text that flows along a curve, optionally scrolling around it — circular badges and arched headings.",
    "description": "Uses <textPath href='#path'> to lay text along an arbitrary path, with an animated startOffset to make it travel — perfect for circular seal/badge text and curved labels.",
    "theme": "svg-effects",
    "categories": ["svg", "typography", "text"],
    "tags": ["svg", "textpath", "curved-text", "badge", "circular", "arc"],
    "tech": ["svg"],
    "era": "timeless",
    "difficulty": "intermediate",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All modern browsers (textPath). startOffset animation via SMIL works broadly.",
    "performance_notes": "Cheap. Define the path once and reference it; hide the path stroke if you only want the text.",
    "accessibility_notes": "SVG text is real, selectable, and readable by screen readers — better than image text. Add role=img + aria-label on the SVG if the curved text is part of a logo. SMIL motion ignores reduced-motion; the script pauses it.",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "path d", "description": "The curve the text follows (arc, circle, wave)", "default": "circle path" },
      { "name": "startOffset", "description": "Where text begins along the path (animate to scroll)", "default": "0%" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Static arched heading", "Rotating circular badge/seal", "Text along a wavy or spiral path"],
    "related": ["svg-line-draw", "rainbow-text"],
    "ai_usage": "Use for circular badges/seals, arched titles, and curved labels. Define a <path id='p'> (a circle drawn as a path for a ring of text, or an arc for an arch) and put <text><textPath href='#p'>…</textPath></text>. Set the path's stroke to none if you only want text. To rotate the text around the ring, animate startOffset 0%→100% (SMIL) — pause under reduced motion. SVG text stays accessible/selectable."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; }
    text { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; fill: #2dd4bf; letter-spacing: 2px; }
    .ring { fill: none; stroke: #1b2030; stroke-width: 1; }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <svg width="240" height="240" viewBox="0 0 240 240" role="img" aria-label="Style Catalog circular badge">
    <path id="circle" class="ring" d="M120,120 m-90,0 a90,90 0 1,1 180,0 a90,90 0 1,1 -180,0" />
    <text font-size="18">
      <textPath href="#circle" startOffset="0%">
        ★ STYLE CATALOG ★ MUSEUM OF WEB EFFECTS 
        <animate attributeName="startOffset" from="0%" to="100%" dur="12s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
      </textPath>
    </text>
    <text x="120" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" fill="#7c5cff">SVG</text>
  </svg>
  <script>
    if (matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches) {
      document.querySelectorAll("svg").forEach(function (s) { if (s.pauseAnimations) s.pauseAnimations(); });
    }
  </script>
</body>
</html>
